body:
## Summary
- Add validated planner support for `next_action: orchestrate` with required child lanes.
- Thread orchestrator lane and chain inputs through orchestrator, implement, review, and fix-pr workflows.
- Dispatch child orchestrators in bounded heuristic mode and document the meta/child orchestration model.

## Verification
- `npm test`

Closes #51

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #61 for issue #51; an automated review is the appropriate next step before merge or further fixes.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777531611115 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNTEyNjM4ODE6aW1wbGVtZW50OjUxOnJldmlldzo2MToz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 91 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` applies `applyDelegatedRouteAuthorization()` after planner/manual decisions, and child `agent-orchestrator.yml` dispatch preserves `requested_by`, `author_association`, `access_policy`, and `repository_private`. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Docs keep direct `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr` one-shot, while child orchestrators are launched only from explicit `/orchestrate` context and are forced into bounded `heuristics` mode. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR extends existing `handoff.ts`, `orchestrate-handoff.ts`, `dispatchWorkflow()`, marker dedupe, and workflow handback paths rather than adding a separate orchestration implementation. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updated `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`, and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` for lane/child orchestration behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds separate `orchestrator_lane`, `parent_orchestrator_lane`, `orchestration_chain_id`, and `orchestrator_context` workflow/planner fields instead of overloading request text or a combined string. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body closes #51, and the diff implements the issue’s discussed lane-based child orchestrator model with #30-style base propagation retained. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are confined to orchestration docs, prompt, handoff types/CLI, workflow inputs, and focused tests. No broad refactor or unrelated cleanup appears in the diff. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation supports one child orchestrator lane at a time, dispatches children in `heuristics` mode, and reuses the existing bounded `implement -> review -> fix-pr -> review` loop. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | partial | 4/6 | Existing marker comments are created for dispatched handoffs, but new child-orchestrator guardrail stops such as missing `orchestrator_lane` only set workflow outputs/logs and do not create a thread-visible stop comment. |

## Notes

- Strongest rubric alignment: explicit structured lane inputs, preservation of downstream authorization context, reuse of the existing bounded handoff machinery, and synced documentation.
- Main rubric-specific gap: expected stop states in the new child-orchestrator planner path are visible in outputs/logs, but not posted as concise thread-visible status comments.
- CI evidence: `gh pr checks` reports `check` passing.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** For `comment-on-accepted-workflow-stops`, planner guardrail stops introduced or exercised by this PR, for example `agent planner requested orchestrate without orchestrator_lane`, exit cleanly with outputs but no visible issue/PR comment. A small follow-up should post/update a concise status when an accepted orchestrator run stops due to policy, malformed planner output, or budget.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #61 implements issue #51’s meta/child orchestrator model, allowing an `agent`-mode planner to dispatch `next_action: orchestrate`.
- It adds orchestrator lanes, chain IDs, child-orchestrator workflow inputs, dedupe lane keys, documentation, prompt updates, and regression tests.
- The intended model is issue-level meta orchestration dispatching bounded child lanes such as `stage-1`, where each child runs the existing implement/review/fix-pr loop and then reports back to the meta lane.
- The current implementation creates child lanes, but reviewer feedback indicates the child’s staged work context and terminal report-back path are incomplete.

## Review
- The implementation is directionally aligned with the requested model, and the rubric review found strong alignment on authorization, structured inputs, reuse, docs, and surgical scope.
- However, the code review identified two blocking gaps in the actual orchestration behavior: child context is not carried into the child’s work, and child completion does not resume the parent/meta orchestrator.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Child stage context is dropped | BLOCKING | Child orchestrators are forced into `heuristics` mode, but the provided `orchestrator_context` is not forwarded into the first implement/review action, so staged instructions from the meta planner are lost. |
| Child lanes cannot report back to the meta lane | BLOCKING | `parent_orchestrator_lane` is only passed to the initial child orchestrator run, not through child action runs or terminal handoff behavior, so the meta orchestrator cannot resume for the next stage. |
| Guardrail stops are not thread-visible | WARNING | New planner guardrail stops, such as missing `orchestrator_lane`, stop cleanly via outputs/logs but do not post a concise visible status comment. |

## Progress
- Rubric review passed overall and confirmed delegated authorization context is preserved for the initial child orchestrator dispatch.
- Docs and prompts were updated to describe configurable lanes and child orchestration.
- Existing tests cover meta -> child workflow dispatch and required child lane validation.
- I posted inline comments for the two blocking file/line-specific issues; no prior inline comments existed.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Child stage context is dropped</summary>

**Cause:** The child orchestrator dispatch sets `automation_mode: "heuristics"` and passes `orchestrator_context`, but the child then follows deterministic routing. The subsequent `implement` dispatch does not pass that context into `agent-implement.yml`, so the child’s stage-specific instructions are not available to the implementation prompt.

**Candidate solutions:** Either allow the child planner to consume `orchestrator_context`, or add explicit child context inputs to implement/review/fix-pr prompts and pass them through the action dispatch path. Add coverage for meta -> child -> implement context propagation.

**Comments:** This was posted inline on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:400`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Child lanes cannot report back to the meta lane</summary>

**Cause:** `parent_orchestrator_lane` is preserved only on the initial child orchestrator workflow dispatch. The child’s later implement/review/fix-pr runs preserve only the child lane and chain ID, and terminal outcomes do not dispatch the parent/meta lane again.

**Candidate solutions:** Thread parent-lane context through child action workflows and `dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts`, then add terminal child -> parent handoff behavior for SHIP, blocked, budget exhaustion, and failure states.

**Comments:** This was posted inline on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:413`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Guardrail stops are not thread-visible</summary>

**Cause:** Planner guardrail stops introduced by this PR return clean workflow outputs and logs, but they do not create or update a concise visible PR/issue status comment.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a visible stop comment path for accepted orchestrator runs that stop due to malformed planner output, policy denial, target state, or budget exhaustion. Keep hidden dedupe markers for dispatch safety, but surface expected stop reasons to the thread.

**Comments:** The rubric review already called this out as a warning in the PR conversation.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the two blocking findings require concrete branch changes and are suitable for an automated fix-pr pass with focused regression tests.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Preserve child `orchestrator_context` into the child’s actual implement/review/fix-pr work path.
- [ ] Preserve parent-lane context through child action runs and dispatch the parent/meta orchestrator when a child lane reaches a terminal state.
- [ ] Add regression coverage for meta -> child -> implement context propagation and child terminal result -> parent orchestrator dispatch.
- [ ] Surface visible status comments for accepted orchestrator runs that stop at new planner guardrails.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis verdict is NEEDS_REWORK with Recommended Next Step FIX_PR; two blocking orchestration behavior gaps require concrete branch changes and focused tests.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `review` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 84 | PARTIAL | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` applies delegated authorization after planner/manual selection, and child `agent-orchestrator.yml` dispatch preserves `requested_by`, `author_association`, `access_policy`, and `repository_private`. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Direct `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr` remain documented as one-shot; child orchestrators are only launched from orchestrator context and forced into `heuristics` mode. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 5/7 | The PR adds explicit `orchestrator_lane`, `parent_orchestrator_lane`, `orchestration_chain_id`, and `orchestrator_context` fields, but `parent_orchestrator_lane` is not threaded through implement/review/fix-pr workflows, limiting the explicit parent-report-back contract described in #51. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation extends existing `handoff.ts`, `orchestrate-handoff.ts`, `dispatchWorkflow()`, marker dedupe, and workflow handback paths instead of adding a parallel orchestration mechanism. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`, and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` were updated for child lanes and planner `next_action: orchestrate`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | partial | 3/6 | The PR implements the lane concept from #51, but linked issue discussion explicitly asked for child completion to report back to the meta orchestrator and visible chain state; the diff does not implement those parts. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | partial | 2/6 | New guardrail stops such as missing child `orchestrator_lane` are covered by tests as clean `decision=stop` outputs, but no thread-visible stop/status comment is added. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are limited to orchestration docs, prompt, handoff types/CLI, workflow inputs, and focused tests. No unrelated refactor is visible in the PR diff. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The documentation changes describe orchestration behavior and topology without adding per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The PR keeps child orchestration bounded to one child lane at a time and reuses the existing `implement -> review -> fix-pr -> review` loop in `heuristics` mode. |

## Notes

- Strong rubric alignment: authorization context is preserved for child dispatch, structured lane fields are introduced, docs/prompts are updated, and the implementation stays scoped.
- Main rubric gaps: the linked #51 model included visible chain state and child-to-meta report-back; this PR implements lane handoff setup but not the full parent continuation path.
- Verification evidence in the PR body says `npm test`; I did not rerun tests during this rubric-only review.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** For `comment-on-accepted-workflow-stops`, planner guardrail stops introduced by this PR only surface through workflow outputs/logs. Add a concise visible issue/PR status when an accepted orchestrator run stops due to malformed planner output, policy/budget guardrails, or other expected stops.
- **WARNING:** For `prefer-explicit-structured-inputs` and `read-docs-first`, `parent_orchestrator_lane` exists on the child orchestrator dispatch but is not propagated through child action workflows, so a child lane cannot explicitly report terminal state back to the meta lane as requested in #51.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #61 implements issue #51’s meta/child orchestrator model by adding `next_action: orchestrate`, configurable orchestrator lanes, chain IDs, and child workflow dispatch.
- The goal is for an issue-level meta orchestrator to split work into bounded child lanes, each running the existing implement/review/fix-pr loop.
- The PR also updates workflows, prompts, docs, and tests for lane-aware orchestration and stacked base propagation.
- Reviews agree the implementation is directionally aligned, but several core orchestration contracts are incomplete.

## Review
- The PR is not ready to ship because the child orchestration path can lose stage context, cannot report terminal state back to the meta lane, and can fall back from invalid planner output into mutation.
- The rubric review rated some gaps as warnings, while the code review treats parent report-back and child context propagation as blocking because they are central to issue #51’s requested model.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Child stage context is dropped | BLOCKING | Child orchestrators receive `orchestrator_context`, but because they run in `heuristics` mode, that context is not forwarded into the actual implement/review/fix-pr work. |
| Child lanes cannot report back to the meta lane | BLOCKING | `parent_orchestrator_lane` is only present on the initial child dispatch, so terminal child outcomes cannot resume the issue-level meta orchestrator. |
| Invalid planner output can fall back to mutation | BLOCKING | In `agent` mode, a missing or malformed planner response can fall back to deterministic manual orchestration and dispatch implementation instead of stopping clearly. |
| New prompt lane variables render empty | WARNING | The prompt references lane and chain variables that are not allowlisted in `.agent/src/run.ts`, so the planner does not actually see those fields. |
| Guardrail stops are not thread-visible | WARNING | New expected stop paths surface through outputs/logs but do not post concise visible status comments on the issue or PR. |

## Progress
- Delegated authorization context is preserved for the initial child orchestrator dispatch.
- Docs and prompts were updated for child lanes and planner `next_action: orchestrate`.
- Tests cover initial meta-to-child dispatch and required child lane validation.
- Existing inline comments already covered child context loss and missing parent report-back.
- I posted new inline comments for invalid planner fallback and missing prompt variable allowlisting.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Child stage context is dropped</summary>

**Cause:** The meta planner’s `handoff_context` is passed to the child orchestrator as `orchestrator_context`, but the child is forced into `heuristics` mode and then dispatches action workflows through inputs that do not carry that context into the work prompts.

**Candidate solutions:** Thread explicit child context through implement/review/fix-pr inputs and prompts, or allow the child planner path to consume `orchestrator_context`. Add regression coverage for meta -> child -> implement context propagation.

**Comments:** Already posted inline on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Child lanes cannot report back to the meta lane</summary>

**Cause:** The parent lane is not preserved through child action workflows, and terminal outcomes such as SHIP, blocked, failure, or budget exhaustion stop the child chain instead of dispatching the parent/meta orchestrator.

**Candidate solutions:** Add parent-lane context to child action workflows and handback CLIs, then dispatch the parent orchestrator when child lanes reach terminal states. Cover SHIP, blocked/failure, and budget-stop paths.

**Comments:** Rubrics classified this as a warning, but the synthesis treats it as blocking because issue #51 explicitly requested child completion to report back to the meta orchestrator.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Invalid planner output can fall back to mutation</summary>

**Cause:** `usePlannerDecision` is false when planner output is missing or malformed, so manual `/orchestrate` in `agent` mode can fall back to `decideManualOrchestration()` and dispatch implementation.

**Candidate solutions:** Track that planner mode was attempted and stop with an explicit planner-invalid reason when output is absent or invalid. Add a malformed planner-output regression test.

**Comments:** Posted inline on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:257`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>New prompt lane variables render empty</summary>

**Cause:** `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` references `ORCHESTRATOR_LANE`, `ORCHESTRATOR_PARENT_LANE`, and `ORCHESTRATION_CHAIN_ID`, but `.agent/src/run.ts` does not include them in `SUPPLEMENTAL_PROMPT_VAR_NAMES`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add the new variables to the allowlist and add prompt-rendering coverage so future workflow prompt fields cannot silently render empty.

**Comments:** Posted inline on `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md:15`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Guardrail stops are not thread-visible</summary>

**Cause:** Guardrail stops such as malformed planner output, missing child lane, policy denial, or budget exhaustion currently surface through logs/outputs rather than a concise thread-visible status.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a visible stop-status comment path for accepted orchestrator runs that stop due to expected guardrails, while keeping hidden markers for dedupe.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: unresolved findings require concrete branch changes and are suitable for an automated fix-pr pass with focused regression tests.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Preserve child `orchestrator_context` into the child’s actual implement/review/fix-pr work path.
- [ ] Preserve parent-lane context through child action runs and dispatch the parent/meta orchestrator on child terminal states.
- [ ] Stop clearly on missing or malformed planner output after planner mode is attempted.
- [ ] Add the new orchestrator lane and chain prompt variables to the runtime prompt allowlist.
- [ ] Surface visible status comments for accepted orchestrator runs that stop at expected guardrails.
- [ ] Add regression coverage for child context propagation, child terminal report-back, malformed planner output, and rendered prompt lane variables.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis verdict is NEEDS_REWORK with Recommended Next Step FIX_PR; unresolved blocking findings require branch changes and focused regression tests.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777560358373 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzE2Njc4OTk6cmV2aWV3OjYxOmZpeF9wcjo2MToz -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-51/codex-25151263881`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Threaded child orchestrator context into implement/review/fix-pr action paths and prompts, including parent lane, chain id, and original orchestrator target for terminal report-back.
- Added child terminal report-back to the parent/meta orchestrator lane and made missing/malformed agent planner output stop clearly instead of falling back to mutation.
- Added `ORCHESTRATOR_LANE`, `ORCHESTRATOR_PARENT_LANE`, and `ORCHESTRATION_CHAIN_ID` to prompt rendering, updated docs, and added focused regression coverage.
- Verification: `npm run build && node --test dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js` passed; `npm test` still fails in existing discussion post gate shell tests because `scripts/resolve-discussion-post-gate.sh` is not found from the `.agent` test cwd.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr completed successfully after the latest review synthesis requested concrete branch changes; an automated review should verify the fixes and regression coverage before stopping.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 96 | PASS | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Child handoffs preserve requester policy fields and re-check downstream route access in [`applyDelegatedRouteAuthorization`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af7b05ba8b678c0a7c8327e53c64d0ed3aefa8d5/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L239). Child dispatch forwards `author_association`, `access_policy`, and `repository_private` to `agent-orchestrator.yml`. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Direct action workflows still require explicit `orchestration_enabled`; child orchestrators are launched only from orchestrator context and forced to `automation_mode: "heuristics"` at dispatch. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The PR adds separate typed fields for `orchestrator_lane`, `parent_orchestrator_lane`, `orchestration_chain_id`, `orchestrator_context`, and original target fields instead of encoding lane state in request text. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation extends existing handoff parsing, dedupe markers, `dispatchWorkflow`, and action handback paths rather than creating a parallel orchestration system. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Orchestration docs now describe lane inputs, child `next_action: "orchestrate"`, bounded heuristic child loops, context propagation, and parent/meta report-back. See [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/af7b05ba8b678c0a7c8327e53c64d0ed3aefa8d5/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L95). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | partial | 5/6 | The implementation now covers the core #51 model: meta lane, child lane, stage context, bounded child loop, and report-back. The broader issue discussion also asked for visible chain status/resume semantics, which remain only partially represented. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | partial | 4/6 | Dispatch handoffs still create visible marker comments, but non-dispatch guardrail stops such as malformed planner output or missing child lane stop through outputs/logs without a concise thread-visible stop comment. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to orchestration workflows, handoff/runtime code, prompts, docs, and focused tests. No unrelated broad refactor is visible. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation explains the orchestration model structurally without adding per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The PR keeps child orchestration bounded to one child lane at a time and reuses the existing `implement -> review -> fix-pr -> review` loop in heuristic mode. |

## Notes

- Strong rubric alignment: the latest PR head fixes the earlier context-loss and parent-report-back gaps. Tests now cover child dispatch, required child lane validation, malformed planner stops, child context propagation, and terminal child report-back.
- Verification: `npm test` passed locally after rerunning with build-output write access; result was 375 passing tests.
- Smallest useful follow-up: add/update a concise visible orchestration status comment for expected stop states and chain progress.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The remaining rubric gap is visibility for accepted orchestrator stops. For example, `agent planner requested orchestrate without orchestrator_lane` is covered as a clean stop in tests, but the workflow does not post a thread-visible status explaining that accepted automation stopped at that guardrail.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

title: Support child orchestrator lane handoffs
